headline: Summer Camp is the heart of the Camp Dream GA experience
subheadline: Hosted at the Calvin Center in Hampton, Georgia, Summer Camp offers an overnight camp environment for children and young adults with disabilities through a volunteer-supported, community-centered model.
stepsHeadline: What Summer Camp is designed to provide

steps.title: Arrival and welcome
steps.description: Families arrive to a prepared campus where campers are welcomed into a supportive environment built around participation.

steps.title: Shared camp life
steps.description: Campers experience recreation, music, crafts, meals, worship, and cabin community with volunteer support throughout the session.

steps.title: Confidence and connection
steps.description: The goal is not only a full schedule. It is a camp experience that helps campers feel known, included, and encouraged.

sections.title: Who camp is for
sections.description: Summer Camp is built for children and young adults with disabilities, especially families seeking a traditional camp setting prepared for more individualized support.

sections.title: What camp can include
sections.description: Activities may include swimming, music, crafts, recreation, worship, shared meals, and cabin-based community time.

sections.title: How support works
sections.description: Volunteer counselors and medical volunteers help create a safer, more relational environment so campers can participate more fully.

faq.question: Where is Summer Camp held?
faq.answer: Camp is held at the Calvin Center in Hampton, Georgia, a campus that provides the setting for the overnight summer sessions.

faq.question: Who supports campers during the session?
faq.answer: Volunteer counselors, junior counselors, medical volunteers, and staff work together to help campers participate in camp life.

cta.heading: Read the FAQ for practical details or move into the volunteer flow if you want to help make Summer Camp possible.
cta.body: Families, volunteers, and donors all connect back to Summer Camp as the main expression of the mission.
